title: Scene Visualization & Dynamic Scene Merging
description: Post-generation pipeline for visualizing and merging 3D dynamic scenes produced by a Gaussian-splatting world model (Lyra). Users invoke CLI scripts to subsample, concatenate, and render output MP4 videos from multi-timestep world outputs.

actors.name: User
actors.description: Researcher/engineer triggering visualization scripts via CLI

actors.name: FFmpeg/FFprobe
actors.description: External video-processing tool invoked via subprocess for encoding, filtering, and concatenation

actors.name: OpenCV (cv2)
actors.description: Frame-level video reader/writer used in static-camera pipeline

use_cases.name: Merge Dynamic Scene Segments
use_cases.description: Temporally concatenate matching MP4s from multiple output directories with per-directory frame subsampling (stride). Entry: dynamic_scene_merge.py main()
key_functions: main() → parse_args() → process_and_concat(input_dirs, strides, output_dir)
process_and_concat: discovers common MP4 filenames across dirs, calls subsample_one per dir, then ffmpeg concat
subsample_one(input_path, stride, temp_path, target_fps): ffmpeg -vf select/setpts/fps filter, drops audio
ffprobe_fps(path): probes avg_frame_rate via ffprobe subprocess, fallback r_frame_rate, default 30.0
use_cases.outputs: Concatenated MP4 files written to output_dir (same filenames as inputs)

use_cases.name: Generate Static-Camera Timeline Videos
use_cases.description: Collect frames across timestep folders (time_idx = 0..max) for a fixed camera view and write per-video-index MP4 outputs. Entry: dynamic_scene_video_static_camera.py __main__
key_functions: collect_video_indices(main_path, sub_path, max_time_idx): globs rgb_*_view_idx_0_0.4.mp4 across time folders
collect_frames(main_path, sub_path, video_indices, num_frames, max_time_idx): cv2.VideoCapture per video
save_concatenated_videos(frames_dict, output_path, fps): cv2.VideoWriter with avc1 → mp4v fallback
use_cases.inputs: main_path, sub_path, output_path, num_frames, fps, max_time_idx

use_cases.name: Create Teaser/Wave/Grid Videos
use_cases.description: Build wave (looping), single-view concatenated, and optional grid layout videos from rendered output directories. Entry: dynamic_scene_video_static_scene.py __main__
key_functions: create_teaser_video(mp4_paths_or_dir, wave_output_dir, single_output_dir, grid_output_dir, grid_rows, grid_cols, use_mirror_views, use_mirror_wave, fps)
find_subvideos(main_path): parses sample_idx/param from filename, finds wave and view-index MP4s
Wave stage: ffmpeg filter_complex concat with optional reverse (mirror) for each wave video
Single stage: ffmpeg filter_complex concat of view-index videos with optional mirror
Grid stage: ffmpeg hstack + vstack filter for grid_rows×grid_cols batches of single videos
natural_sort(strings): natural-order sort for video filenames (rgb_9 < rgb_10)
use_cases.inputs: mp4_input dir (or list), wave_output_dir, single_output_dir, grid_output_dir (optional), mirror flags, grid_rows/cols, fps
use_cases.outputs: Wave MP4s, single (views) MP4s, and optional grid MP4s

use_cases.name: Render 3D Gaussian Scene
use_cases.description: Render a Gaussian splatting scene from camera views. Used upstream by the generation model before visualization scripts consume the MP4 outputs.
key_functions: GaussianRenderer.__init__(opt)
GaussianRenderer.render(gaussians, cam_view, bg_color=None, intrinsics=None)
GaussianRenderer.save_ply(gaussians, path, compatible=True)
GaussianRenderer.load_ply(path, compatible=True)
use_cases.notes: GaussianRendererDeferred (gs_deferred.py) extends this with render_deferred and render_standard methods for patch-based deferred rendering

use_cases.name: Reconstruct Latent 3D Gaussians
use_cases.description: Predict 3D Gaussian parameters from multi-view images and temporal embeddings. Produces gaussians fed into GaussianRenderer.
key_functions: LatentRecon.__init__(opt)
LatentRecon.forward(data, skip_loss=False): full training/inference forward pass
LatentRecon.forward_gaussians(images_input, plucker_embedding, rays_os, rays_ds, time_embeddings, num_input_multi_views=None)
LatentRecon.gaussian_processing(x: Tensor, rays_os: Tensor, rays_ds: Tensor)
LatentRecon.get_time_embedding(time_embeddings: Tensor, V: int, num_input_multi_views: int=None)
LatentRecon.gaussian_pruning(gaussians: Tensor)
LatentRecon.subsample_x_and_rays_wrapper(x, rays_os, rays_ds, x_mask)
use_cases.notes: Outputs Gaussian parameters (positions, opacities, scales, rotations, colors) consumed by GaussianRenderer.render()

use_case_relationships: UC3 <<include>> Build Single View Videos (ffmpeg concat)
UC1, UC2, UC3 consume MP4 outputs produced by UC4+UC5 pipeline
key_relationships: LatentRecon uses GaussianRenderer (composition via opt config)
GaussianRenderer depends_on GaussianRendererDeferred (deferred variant, same interface)
All visu scripts consume MP4 files output by GaussianRenderer.render() in generation step
important_notes: All visualization scripts are pure post-processing CLI tools — they do NOT call LatentRecon or GaussianRenderer directly; they operate on pre-rendered MP4 files stored under timestep-indexed directories (e.g., main_path/0/sub_path/rgb_*.mp4). Video encoding: H.264/libx264, CRF 18, yuv420p. Temporary files cleaned up in dynamic_scene_merge. Pattern for output filenames: rgb_{sample_idx}_view_idx_*_{param}.mp4.
